chunks.text: The Electrical Engineering Computer Science Division offers advising services for undergraduate students regarding degree requirements, enrollment, and resources. We are here to help and support you through your studies. Please feel free to contact us if you have any questions related to the EECS major, coursework, research, or just need someone to talk to.

In-person Drop-in Advising availabile Tuesday/Wednesday 1:30 – 3:30 PM. CS Students: 349 Soda. EECS Students: 205 Cory. Virtual Drop-in advising is available on Thursdays, 1:30 – 3:30 pm. Book an Appointment (Zoom). Need to chat with an advisor? Use our online booking system to schedule a 1:1 appointment. Book Zoom Appointment (EECS Only). You can always reach us during business hours by email, cs-advising@berkeley.edu (CS students) or eecs-advising@berkeley.edu (EECS students).

EECS Vs. ESS. Use the table below to determine whether an EECS or ESS adviser is better suited to support your specific advising needs. EECS Department: College of Engineering. A.K.A. EECS Adviser or Major Adviser. Location: 205 Cory Hall. Experts on: Anything related to the EECS major or minor. Questions to Ask: Program Planning, Ethics requirement, Enrollment questions, EE and CS course information, Undergraduate Research Internships, Graduate school planning, Department programs (honors programs, student organizations, workshops, etc), EECS Minor.

ESS Adviser or College adviser. Location: 120 Grimes Engineering Center. Experts on: EECS major and non-major related questions, college policies. Questions to Ask: Curricular Practical Training (CPT), Degree checks, H/SS, Withdrawing & Readmission, Double majors, etc, Program Planning, Petitions, Course units outside of the usual load, Optional Practical Training (OPT), Exception petitions for EECS requirements, Study abroad.

EECS Advisors. Gina Garcia: EECS Student Advisor, 205 Cory, +1 (510) 642-7644, eecs-advising@berkeley.edu. EECS and Prospective students: Book an appointment with Gina. Kait Guthrie: EECS Student Advisor, 205 Cory, +1 (510) 642-1786, eecs-advising@berkeley.edu. EECS and Prospective students: Book an appointment with Kait. Grayson Johnston: eECS Student Advisor, 205 Cory, +1 (510) 642-7372, eecs-advising@berkeley.edu. EECS and Prospective students: Book an appointment with Grayson.

EE Class Scheduling and Enrollment. Kait Guthrie: EE Scheduler and Advisor, 205 Cory, +1 (510) 642-1786, ee-scheduling@berkeley.edu. EECS and Prospective students: Book an appointment with Kait. Getting Into EE Classes.

ESS Peer Advising. Peer advisers provide general information to engineering undergraduates about university and college requirements and procedures concerning registration, deadlines, research and leadership opportunities, student organizations, campus resources and special events. Peer advisers also meet with prospective students and lead workshops throughout each semester.
